title: Separator sizing classes never match Base UI orientation attributes
reasoning: The wrapper uses Tailwind variants for data-horizontal and data-vertical attributes, but the Base UI separator API exposes orientation through data-orientation. As a result, the horizontal and vertical dimension utilities do not apply; the default separator renders with only shrink-0 and bg-border, so it can collapse to no visible size.
reproduction: Render <Separator /> or <Separator orientation="vertical" /> and inspect the generated element/classes. The element receives an orientation prop, but the CSS selectors in the class string target data-horizontal/data-vertical instead of data-orientation values, so the height/width utilities are inactive.
recommendation: Change the variants to target the actual orientation attribute, for example data-[orientation=horizontal]:h-px data-[orientation=horizontal]:w-full data-[orientation=vertical]:w-px data-[orientation=vertical]:self-stretch, or use the Base UI state className callback to branch on orientation.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are listed for this component, and there is no snapshot or DOM-style assertion covering the classes applied for horizontal and vertical separators.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a component test that renders horizontal and vertical separators and asserts that the generated className includes selectors for data-[orientation=horizontal] and data-[orientation=vertical], or verifies the rendered element has nonzero dimensions under the app's Tailwind output.
minimumFixScope: Update frontend/src/components/ui/separator.tsx className variants and add a focused separator rendering/style test.
